pattern: The answer is bottlenecked by the scarcest required letter once each letter's supply is divided by how many copies one target word needs.
hints: Each finished word consumes a fixed recipe of letters; only the letters appearing in the target word matter.
Count how many of each needed letter you have, but remember some letters are needed more than once per word.
For every distinct letter in the target, divide its available count by its required-per-word count; the smallest of those floored ratios is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: enough for two words with leftovers
single o limits despite plenty of l
all the needed letters but one short on a doubled letter
irrelevant letters padding a valid word

stress.description: long random lowercase text mixing relevant and irrelevant letters
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    n = max(1, size)
    pool = 'balloon' * 5 + string.ascii_lowercase
    return [''.join(rng.choice(pool) for _ in range(n))]
stress.note: weighting balloon letters keeps the count meaningfully positive
